inference_unit.notations: - $H_{c2}$: upper critical field of a superconductor - $\xi$: coherence length of the superconducting order parameter - $\phi_{0}$: magnetic flux quantum - $v_{F}$: Fermi velocity - $T_{c}$: superconducting critical temperature - $h$: Planck constant - $k_{\mathrm{B}}$: Boltzmann constant - WHH: Werthamer–Helfand–Hohenberg model - GL: Ginzburg–Landau equation

premise.title: Relation between coherence length and upper critical field

premises.premise: The coherence length of the superconducting order parameter, denoted by $\xi$, is determined from the upper critical field $H_{c2}$ using the relation $\xi = \sqrt{\frac{\phi_{0}}{3\pi^{2}H_{c2}}}$, where $\phi_{0}$ is the magnetic flux quantum.

The Fermi velocity $v_{F}$ of charge carriers in a superconductor is related to the coherence length $\xi$ and the critical temperature $T_{c}$ via the Bardeen–Cooper–Schrieffer (BCS) relation $\xi = 0.18 \frac{h v_{F}}{k_{\mathrm{B}} T_{c}}$, where $h$ is the Planck constant and $k_{\mathrm{B}}$ is the Boltzmann constant.

assumption.title: Validity of WHH model for upper critical field estimation

premises.assumption: The temperature dependence of the upper critical field $H_{c2}(T)$ for a superconductor can be approximated by the Werthamer–Helfand–Hohenberg (WHH) model across the entire temperature range to provide an estimate of the upper critical field at zero temperature, $H_{c2}(0\text{ K})$. [45]
